rulings.Case Title: DONALD KEITH ANDERS ET AL VS. AMERICAN BILTRITE INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, NOVEMBER 5, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant American Biltrite Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' Firstly Amended Complaint is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff's complaint, including plaintiff's first, second, third and fourth causes of action, states facts sufficient to constitute such causes of action and is not uncertain. Plaintiff's allegations meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Defendant must answer within 10 days. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD KEITH ANDERS ET AL VS. AMERICAN BILTRITE INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant American Biltrite Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Firstly Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, NOVEMBER 5, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant American Biltrite Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiff's Firstly Amended Complaint is denied. Plaintiff's complaint alleges facts sufficient to support a claim for punitive damages. Defendant must answer within 10 days. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
